feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Newly generated RAP artifacts not working [BTP-Trial]
entry.content:
I have always created new RAP applications in BTP Trial from a database table using the "Generate ABAP Repository Objects..." feature. However, in the past few days, after generating the application, I am no longer able to preview it. The error "Could not open app. Please try again later." appears every time I try to run it. Am I missing something? The metadata extension and behavior definitions are ok as far as I know.

@EndUserText.label : 'Users Table'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zjp_users {

  key mandt             : abap.clnt not null;
  key userid            : sysuuid_x16 not null;
  username              : abap.char(20);
  role                  : abap.char(20);
  created_by            : abp_creation_user;
  created_at            : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;
  local_last_change_at  : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
  last_changed_by       : abp_lastchange_user;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

entry.title: Creating Production Order Component Line Items via API
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

We are currently working on a Custom Application Developed using the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) model in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, where Batch wise line items are being managed.

As part of our requirement, we are using the A_ProductionOrderComponent_4 API to update values in the CO02 Production Order screen. However, this API does not fully meet our needs.

At present, when using the PATCH method, we are only able to modify existing line items and are unable to create new ones.

Our requirement is to create new line items in the Production Order Component along with the corresponding batch details. We would like to understand if there is any POST method or appropriate API available to support this functionality. Alternatively, we would appreciate any recommendations for a suitable approach to achieve this requirement.

Thank you,

Production order Component line Item

entry.title: Feature Instance not working in multi select list report
entry.content:
Hello, 

i have the following problem. In my RAP application i have some buttons with feature instance control. In my frontend i get the right settings for each button.  (OdataV2)

Now when i select both lines in the table i would expect that all buttons are disabled. But the button is still enabled even when i select both rows.

What did I do wrong?

entry.title: How to handle a POST call in RAP using custom entity
entry.content:
I have a requirement where I will receive a payload from an external system and create a notification in SAP.
 
The source payload fields are custom fields. Using these  fields, I need to derive all the required data to create the notification in SAP. This also includes implementing necessary validations and error handling.
 
What would be the best approach to handle this scenario in RAP? Should we create a custom entity, an abstract entity, or is there a better recommended approach?
 
In general, we could also create a service in SEGW and handle everything there. However, the goal is to implement this using RAP to stay compliant with a cloud-ready solution.
 
Thanks in advance 🙂
 
ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model  ABAP Development  OData

entry.title: How to Configure Remote ATC Between SAP BTP ABAP Environment and On‑Premise S/4HANA (Step‑by‑Step)
entry.content:
Cloud Connector RFC Mapping – Not Sure Which Settings to Use

When adding an on‑premise ABAP system in Cloud Connector (Protocol = RFC), I am asked to choose:

  • With load balancing (SID + message server)
  • Without load balancing (app server + instance number)

I am unsure which one is correct for Remote ATC, and what the exact host/port values should be.

Communication System in BTP ABAP Does Not Show RFC Option

After setting up the Cloud Connector entries, when I create a Communication System in the BTP ABAP Environment, the RFC outbound service is missing, and I cannot create the RFC destination required for Remote ATC.

Object Providers Do Not Appear in Custom Code Migration App

In the Custom Code Analysis Project setup screen,
“Object Provider to Remote System” shows No data available, meaning I cannot continue the Remote ATC configuration.

What Is the Correct End‑to‑End Setup?

I am following SAP’s Remote ATC documentation (Cloud Connector + Communication System + Object Provider), but there seems to be missing prerequisites or configuration steps.

Can someone guide me with the correct end‑to‑end steps?

Specifically:

  • Which RFC configuration should be used in Cloud Connector (load balancing or not)?
  • What are the correct message server / app server details for S/4HANA?
  • How to ensure the Communication System in BTP ABAP recognizes the RFC connection?
  • How to make Object Providers appear under ATC Administration → Setup?

Any screenshots, step-by-step instructions, or clarifications from someone who has successfully configured Remote ATC would be greatly appreciated.

I tried to follow few SAP blogs but they are very old to follow up.

Thank you!

entry.title: How to Configure Remote ATC Between SAP BTP ABAP Environment and On‑Premise S/4HANA (Step‑by‑Step)?
entry.content:
* Main issue is that I am not able to  find " Object provider for remote system" under custom code analysis tile.

Cloud Connector RFC Mapping – Not Sure Which Settings to Use

When adding an on‑premise ABAP system in Cloud Connector (Protocol = RFC), I am asked to choose:

  • With load balancing (SID + message server)
  • Without load balancing (app server + instance number)

I am unsure which one is correct for Remote ATC, and what the exact host/port values should be.

Communication System in BTP ABAP Does Not Show RFC Option

After setting up the Cloud Connector entries, when I create a Communication System in the BTP ABAP Environment, the RFC outbound service is missing, and I cannot create the RFC destination required for Remote ATC.

Object Providers Do Not Appear in Custom Code Migration App

In the Custom Code Analysis Project setup screen,
“Object Provider to Remote System” shows No data available, meaning I cannot continue the Remote ATC configuration.

What Is the Correct End‑to‑End Setup?

I am following SAP’s Remote ATC documentation (Cloud Connector + Communication System + Object Provider), but there seems to be missing prerequisites or configuration steps.

Can someone guide me with the correct end‑to‑end steps?

Specifically:

  • Which RFC configuration should be used in Cloud Connector (load balancing or not)?
  • What are the correct message server / app server details for S/4HANA?
  • How to ensure the Communication System in BTP ABAP recognizes the RFC connection?
  • How to make Object Providers appear under ATC Administration → Setup?

Any screenshots, step-by-step instructions, or clarifications from someone who has successfully configured Remote ATC would be greatly appreciated.

I tried to follow few SAP blogs but they are very old to follow up.

Thank you!

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

I must admit that I’m feeling quite stuck custom code analysis catalog inside Analyze custom code analysis tile , as I can’t find any proper end‑to‑end documentation. The SAP Help Portal only provides fragmented information, and none of the pages explain the full process in a clear, sequential way. Could you please share any link or document where all the steps are described in order? Main blocker is when I go inside the catalog Custom Code Analysis -> Custom code analysis tile -> Create custom code analysis project "Object provider to remote system" is blank.How to get it.

entry.title: ABAP Odata
entry.content:
I have completed my OData API project with GET and POST methods for Student, Fee and Library management. The code is working in SAP Gateway Client but I need help with:

(1) I am facing CSRF token validation issue when testing POST request in Gateway Client.
(2) How can I make my API globally accessible so automation team consume API.

i'm using trail server.

entry.title: Seeking Guidance on Next Steps After SAP ABAP Cloud Certification
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I hope you are doing well.

I have recently completed my certification as an SAP Certified Associate – Back-End Developer (ABAP Cloud). As a fresher entering the SAP ecosystem, I am eager to build my career in the right direction and align my skills with current industry demand.

I would greatly appreciate your guidance on the next steps I should take. Specifically, I am looking for suggestions on:

  • Which SAP modules or technologies are currently in high demand

  • Any recommended courses or certifications to complement ABAP Cloud

  • Skills or tools (such as SAP BTP, Fiori, RAP, etc.) that would enhance my profile

  • Tips on how to gain practical experience or improve job readiness

Your advice and insights would be extremely valuable in helping me make informed decisions about my career path.

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Venkatesh Pagilla

entry.title: CDS with all Classes implementing an Interface
entry.content:
Hi,

I'd love to make the following work:

I want to provide a RAP-CDS-View that shows a list of all Classes (including Sub-Classes) that implement a certain interface. I already have a hierarchy-view in place for the Classes and their Sub-Classes and of course I have a simple view that provides me the Interfaces any Class directly implements - but as Classes inherit all their parent's Interfaces recursively, I cannot find a way to implement this in CDS - while in ABAP SQL there is a syntax for exactly this problem (SELECT HIERARCHY DESCENDANTS).

Any idea how to solve that? I have already tried to understand what the "DIRECTORY" addition in the DEFINE HIERARCHY syntax does, but the documentation doesn't make any sense to me 😞

Thanks in advance

André

entry.title: RAP: Issue Calling Standard FM IQS4_ADD_DATA_NOTIFICATION (COMMIT restriction)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently developing a RAP-based Fiori application and have a requirement to update data from a custom table in the UI to a notification using the standard function module IQS4_ADD_DATA_NOTIFICATION.

I added a custom button on the UI that retrieves data from the selected line and then calls this function module to perform the update. However, based on RAP architecture, function modules or BAPIs that contain explicit COMMIT WORK or ROLLBACK WORK statements are not allowed. When triggering the action, I encounter a BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT dump.

I am aware of possible approaches such as using a background job or bgPF (background processing framework). However, I am looking for guidance on the most suitable and recommended solution for this specific scenario

Has anyone faced a similar issue or found a suitable workaround for this scenario in RAP?

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices.

Thank you,

entry.title: The execution checks whether call is made within the implementation of behavior definition
entry.content:
Hi everyone.

I'm developing an app using ABAP RAP.

Before code refactoring I have put all of the logic in the Behavior Implementation (BI) Class. There is a part of a method that contains the following:

MODIFY ENTITIES OF my_entity_def IN LOCAL MODE
           ENTITY MyEntity
           CREATE BY \_Assoc
           FIELDS ( PrimaryKeyOfMyEntity AFieldOfTheAssoc)
           AUTO FILL CID
           WITH VALUE #( ( %tky    = VALUE #( PrimaryKeyOfMyEntity = lv_key )
                           %target = VALUE #( ( PrimaryKeyOfMyEntity = lv_key
                                                AFieldOfTheAssoc = lv_some_value) ) ) ).

 Since this piece of code was still in the BI Class, I had no warnings.

But now that class got too long, and refactoring was required. I have to move this piece of code to another class. And then call it from the BI class. The app still works the same as before, but now I get the following warning on the LOCAL word:

The execution checks whether call is made within the implementation of behavior definition ''my_entity_def".

I would like to know the correct way to handle this warning. Thank you all for your help!

EDIT:

When using autocomplete in place of IN LOCAL MODE, I get:

  • PRIVILEDGED
  • or just leave it empty

I tried both variants, but both of them show me a new error:

The field "PrimaryKeyOfMyEntity" of entity "my_entity_def" cannot be modified.

It's weird how this error happens because I'm not modifying PrimaryKeyOfMyEntity, just using it as the parent of my newly created Assoc Entity.

It's likely because in the Behavior Definition for the Assoc Entity, I have

define behavior for my_assoc_entity_def alias Assoc
...
{
  update;
  delete;

  association _Parent;

  field ( numbering : managed, readonly ) AssocUuid;
  field ( readonly ) PrimaryKeyOfMyEntity; //Basically the PK of parent

But that line has to be there, otherwise there is no connection from the assoc entity to its parents.

entry.title: Transporting OAuth Scope for v4 OData service /IWFND/V4_ADMIN
entry.content:
I have created an OData v4 service binding for my RAP service.  I can use transaction /IWFND/V4_ADMIN to publish my service and include it in a customizing request.  I create the required OAuth scope. When I use transaction SCC1N to copy my transport to another client my service is published there.  This is great because it means that my service will be published in downstream systems when the transport is imported.

My issue is with the OAuth scope. I don't see how to include this in a transport, and when I go to the 2nd client I have to create the OAuth scope again.

What is the correct procedure?  Is there a way to transport the OAuth scope, or will I have to create it in all the downstream systems?

I have reviewed SAP Help and can't find an answer to this.  Ditto with KBAs like 3304204 & 3605323.  Any assistance much appreciated

S/4HANA Private Cloud 2023 FPS03

entry.title: Is PO Creation via RAP (EML) using I_PURCHASEORDERTP_2 Possible in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, is it possible to create Purchase Orders through a RAP application using EML by consuming the standard business object interface I_PURCHASEORDERTP_2, based on data received from an external system (e.g., via Excel upload)?

Please let me know if this approach is supported or not.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Use of Custom CDS Entity YY1_PURCHASE Not Permitted in Development System
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to use a custom business object created via the Custom Business Object and Custom CDS View Fiori apps. A table has been generated, and I am attempting to use the corresponding CDS entity YY1_PURCHASE in my development (RAP) scenario.

However, I am getting the error:
“The use of CDS Entity YY1_PURCHASE is not permitted.”

Is it possible to use such custom CDS entities created via key user tools in RAP development? Or is there any restriction in S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Please let me know if this is supported or not.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Error 'Invalid (navigation) property 'SAP__Messages' in $select of sap.ui.model.odata.v4
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have created a custom V4 Odata service based on RAP framework and could see that the preview is working fine. When Fiori team tried building the app on the top of this V4 service, error 'Invalid (navigation) property 'SAP__Messages' 
in $select of sap.ui.model.odata.v4.ODataContextBinding: /XXXX' is coming up. 

We have tried checking in backend but don't see any error logs in /IWFND/ERROR_LOGS.

Could anyone suggest what could be the possible rootcause of the issue.

Thanks.

entry.title: Generate ABAP Repository Objects not shown
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Generate ABAP Repository Objects not shown in my system. Our eclipse version is Version: 2022-06 (4.24.0). Why Generate ABAP Repository Objects not shown?

 

Advance Thanks

entry.title: rap preview with odata v4 works, with odata v2 does not
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have created a rap service on sap cloud trial account. Originally I've used odata v4. When I want to make preview, it shows all data as expected. When I switch to odata V2, it shows just a blank page. No error noticed in error log so far. Does anybody have similar experience? How to overcome this issue?

Thank you in advance.

Regards,

Radim

entry.title: Consuming API in RAP for Business Partner
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am using the Onpremise system where i have built RAP on top of this API for business partner.

In gateway test client service works fine and no issue in it.

I have built the class with required interface of RAP and when i run the class system goes 404 error.

Kindly suggest some idea on this. Thanks.

Issue is as below:

1. In the last statment of this code, when i debug the standard on the list of Entity set...I am not finding ENTITY as BUSINESS_PARTNER .

Am i missing anything on the below code ?

Service is already in active status.

I am not getting the response and it end up with error 404 .

cl_http_client=>create_by_destination( EXPORTING destination = 'NONE'

IMPORTING client = DATA(lo_http_client)

EXCEPTIONS argument_not_found = 1

destination_not_found = 2

destination_no_authority = 3

plugin_not_active = 4

OTHERS = 5 ).

 

 

lo_client_proxy = /iwbep/cl_cp_client_proxy_fact=>create_v2_remote_proxy(

EXPORTING

is_proxy_model_key = VALUE #( repository_id = 'DEFAULT'

proxy_model_id = 'ZABC'

proxy_model_version = '0001' )

io_http_client = lo_http_client

iv_relative_service_root = '/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/' ).

 

" Navigate to the resource

lo_request = lo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( 'BUSINESS_PARTNER' )->create_request_for_read( ).

Regards,

Karthik S

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Trial: Service Definition and Binding Not Working newly Created Package
entry.content:
Hi SAP RAP Champion,

my package under ZLOCAL 



I attempted to create a Service Definition and Service Binding in the package ZFE_ATS_PR. However, when I preview the service binding, it displays a blank screen.

 

I created a similar Service Definition and Service Binding using the same CDS root entity (without creating a new CDS root) in another package, ZFE_TRAVEL_000405, which was generated by the class ZDMO_CL_FE_TRAVEL_GENERATOR(https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/fiori-tools-rap-prepare-service..html), and it works as expected.

Additionally, when I run the newly created artifacts (Service Definition → Service Binding) in the package created by the autogenerated class, they work correctly. I also tried previewing the service binding from another user’s package, and it works fine there as well.

To troubleshoot further, I deleted my ABAP Cloud Trial cockpit account and recreated it, but the issue still persists. am missing any configuration, authorization, or package-related setting?

Your valuable suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,
Pradhan

entry.title: Can We Build Live Insights Using CDS Views and SAP Analytics Cloud Within 15 Days?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

My name is E Pranith Kumar, and I am a fresher. My team and I are eager to take up a meaningful challenge and deliver a strong outcome within a short timeline.

We are exploring the possibility of building live insights using CDS Views and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). I would like to understand:

  • Is it feasible to build live analytical insights using CDS Views and SAC within 15 days?
  • If yes, what are the key prerequisites (system setup, authorizations, versions, and connectivity)?
  • Which T-codes/tools are typically used for:
    • Creating and exposing CDS Views
    • Enabling live connectivity to SAC
    • Authorization and data access

We have been provided with a few use cases (attached below). Since we are new to this domain, your guidance would be extremely helpful:

  • Which use case would be best suited for a short timeline?
  • Which one offers the best feature scope and business impact?
  • What SAP modules are typically involved for the recommended use case?
  • What type of system access or roles would be required to implement it?

Additionally, if possible, could you please recommend any learning resources or documentation that would help us research, learn, and implement this effectively?

Although we are new to CDS Views and SAC, we are highly motivated and ready to put in the effort. With your guidance, we are confident we can deliver meaningful insights.

Thank you very much for your time and support. I truly appreciate any direction you can provide.

Best regards,
E Pranith Kumar
